brief_justification: Correct answer (Yes) with accurate minute-by-minute trace peaking at 22°C using the 1.5°C/min net rate the problem specifies; goes beyond the answer to explain the overshoot mechanism and derives a general formula (overshoot = heating rate × delay), tracing second-order effects of the lag; proceeds linearly without flagging assumptions or checking its own approach mid-analysis; the trace table itself constitutes implicit verification but no independent re-derivation or boundary test is applied; the problem contains an internal contradiction (net 2°C/min vs. net 1.5°C/min) which the agent silently resolves without flagging the ambiguity or stating which interpretation was chosen; no alternative interpretations (e.g., using 2°C/min net as stated in the first clause) are mentioned or evaluated; every step is shown in the table with clear causal annotations, making the full chain reproducible by a third party.
